MODEL:      - ChainConfig does not decide what is true \u2014 the signer does.      - Consumers must hardcode (or otherwise deterministically choose) the trusted signer per key/namespace they read.      - There is no owner, admin, upgrade path, or pause switch.WRITE NAMESPACES:      - Each `signer` address is its own isolated namespace; the same key under two different signers stores two independent values.      - `write_config()` writes under `msg.sender` (EOAs, DAOs, timelocks, contracts).      - `write_config_as()` writes under `signer` after verifying an ECDSA or EIP-1271 signature.KEY ENCODING:      - Keys are signed as strings (\u2264 32 bytes, non-empty) for wallet readability.      - Keys are stored as `bytes32` (left-justified ASCII).      - Each (signer, key) holds one value type at a time. The signer can change it in any later write.","events":{"EIP712DomainChanged()":{"details":"MAY be emitted to signal that the domain could have changed."}},"kind":"dev","methods":{"__OFF_CHAIN__hash_config((uint256,uint256,(string,address)[],(string,bytes32)[],(string,uint256)[]))":{"details":"Frontends and signing libraries use this to confirm their local digest computation matches the on-chain one before requesting a wallet signature.ERROR CODES:      - `EmptyKey()` if any entry key is the empty string.      - `KeyTooLong(key, length)` if any entry key exceeds 32 bytes.","params":{"config":"Config payload to hash."},"returns":{"digest":"The digest that `write_config_as` will verify against."}},"eip712Domain()":{"details":"See {IERC-5267}."},"read_address(address,bytes32)":{"details":"Prefer this overload inside constructors to avoid string allocation costs.ERROR CODES:      - `KeyNotSet(signer, key)` if no value has ever been written for this (signer, key).      - `KeyTypeMismatch(signer, key, current_value_type, expected_value_type)` if the key is stored under a different type.","params":{"key":"Left-justified ASCII key bytes, e.g. `bytes32(\"USDC_ADDRESS\")`.","signer":"Namespace owner whose value is being read."},"returns":{"value":"Address stored at (signer, key)."}},"read_address(address,string)":{"details":"STRING vs BYTES32 KEY OVERLOADS:      Prefer string overloads for off-chain calls and inspection. Prefer the `bytes32` overload inside constructors to avoid string allocation costs.ERROR CODES:      - `KeyNotSet(signer, key)` if no value has ever been written for this (signer, key).      - `KeyTypeMismatch(signer, key, current_value_type, expected_value_type)` if the key is stored under a different type.      - `EmptyKey()` if `key` is the empty string.      - `KeyTooLong(key, length)` if `key` exceeds 32 bytes.","params":{"key":"Human-readable key (\u2264 32 bytes, non-empty), e.g. `\"USDC_ADDRESS\"`.","signer":"Namespace owner whose value is being read."},"returns":{"value":"Address stored at (signer, key)."}},"read_bytes32(address,bytes32)":{"details":"Prefer this overload inside constructors to avoid string allocation costs.ERROR CODES:      - `KeyNotSet(signer, key)` if no value has ever been written for this (signer, key).      - `KeyTypeMismatch(signer, key, current_value_type, expected_value_type)` if the key is stored under a different type.","params":{"key":"Left-justified ASCII key bytes.","signer":"Namespace owner whose value is being read."},"returns":{"value":"Bytes32 stored at (signer, key)."}},"read_bytes32(address,string)":{"details":"ERROR CODES:      - `KeyNotSet(signer, key)` if no value has ever been written for this (signer, key).      - `KeyTypeMismatch(signer, key, current_value_type, expected_value_type)` if the key is stored under a different type.      - `EmptyKey()` if `key` is the empty string.      - `KeyTooLong(key, length)` if `key` exceeds 32 bytes.","params":{"key":"Human-readable key (\u2264 32 bytes, non-empty).","signer":"Namespace owner whose value is being read."},"returns":{"value":"Bytes32 stored at (signer, key)."}},"read_uint(address,bytes32)":{"details":"Prefer this overload inside constructors to avoid string allocation costs.ERROR CODES:      - `KeyNotSet(signer, key)` if no value has ever been written for this (signer, key).      - `KeyTypeMismatch(signer, key, current_value_type, expected_value_type)` if the key is stored under a different type.","params":{"key":"Left-justified ASCII key bytes.","signer":"Namespace owner whose value is being read."},"returns":{"value":"Uint256 stored at (signer, key)."}},"read_uint(address,string)":{"details":"ERROR CODES:      - `KeyNotSet(signer, key)` if no value has ever been written for this (signer, key).      - `KeyTypeMismatch(signer, key, current_value_type, expected_value_type)` if the key is stored under a different type.      - `EmptyKey()` if `key` is the empty string.      - `KeyTooLong(key, length)` if `key` exceeds 32 bytes.","params":{"key":"Human-readable key (\u2264 32 bytes, non-empty).","signer":"Namespace owner whose value is being read."},"returns":{"value":"Uint256 stored at (signer, key)."}},"write_config((uint256,uint256,(string,address)[],(string,bytes32)[],(string,uint256)[]))":{"details":"TIMESTAMP SEMANTICS:      - `config.timestamp` must be <= `block.timestamp` (no future-dated writes).      - `config.timestamp` must be strictly greater than the prior timestamp recorded for every (signer, key) being touched.      - A key's value AND its type are both mutable across writes \u2014 the signer is trusted with full authority within their namespace, gated only by strict-monotonic timestamps.EMITTED EVENTS:      - `AddressWritten(signer, key, value)` per address entry.      - `Bytes32Written(signer, key, value)` per bytes32 entry.      - `UintWritten(signer, key, value)` per uint entry.ERROR CODES:      - `EmptyConfig()` if the payload has no entries across all three types.      - `ChainIdMismatch(signed_chain_id, block_chain_id)` if `config.chain_id != block.chainid`.      - `TimestampInFuture(signed_timestamp, block_timestamp)` if `config.timestamp > block.timestamp`.      - `StaleConfig(signer, key, signed_timestamp, prev_timestamp)` if the timestamp does not strictly increase.      - `EmptyKey()` if any entry key is the empty string.      - `KeyTooLong(key, length)` if any entry key exceeds 32 bytes.","params":{"config":"Config payload \u2014 chain id, signer-attested timestamp, and entries by type."}},"write_config_as((uint256,uint256,(string,address)[],(string,bytes32)[],(string,uint256)[]),address,bytes,bool)":{"details":"RELAYED AUTHORIZATION:      - Anyone may call this \u2014 `msg.sender` has no authority over the resulting state.      - All entries are written under `signer`, not under `msg.sender`.      - The relayer cannot alter the payload without invalidating the signature.EIP-712 DOMAIN:      - name:               \"ChainConfig\"      - version:            \"1\"      - chainId:            `block.chainid`      - verifyingContract:  ChainConfig address      The signed `Config` also carries `chain_id`; the contract rejects payloads where `config.chain_id != block.chainid`.      This double-binding catches mismatches between payload creation and the wallet's signing context.EMITTED EVENTS:      - Same as `write_config`.ERROR CODES:      - `InvalidSignature(signer, digest, signature)` if ECDSA or EIP-1271 verification fails.      - All error codes from `write_config` apply.","params":{"config":"Config payload \u2014 chain id, signer-attested timestamp, and entries by type.","is_eip1271":"`true` for contract signers (Safe / DAO / EIP-1271); `false` for EOA and EIP-7702 ECDSA.","signature":"EIP-712 signature over the digest returned by `__OFF_CHAIN__hash_config`.","signer":"Namespace owner whose entries are being written (the EIP-712 message signer)."}}},"title":"ChainConfig","version":1},"userdoc":{"kind":"user","methods":{"DOMAIN_SEPARATOR()":{"notice":"EIP-712 domain separator bound to this deployment."},"__OFF_CHAIN__hash_config((uint256,uint256,(string,address)[],(string,bytes32)[],(string,uint256)[]))":{"notice":"Compute the EIP-712 digest of a `Config` payload for off-chain signing."},"read_address(address,bytes32)":{"notice":"Read an address value from `signer`'s namespace using a `bytes32` key."},"read_address(address,string)":{"notice":"Read an address value from `signer`'s namespace using a string key."},"read_bytes32(address,bytes32)":{"notice":"Read a bytes32 value from `signer`'s namespace using a `bytes32` key."},"read_bytes32(address,string)":{"notice":"Read a bytes32 value from `signer`'s namespace using a string key."},"read_uint(address,bytes32)":{"notice":"Read a uint256 value from `signer`'s namespace using a `bytes32` key."},"read_uint(address,string)":{"notice":"Read a uint256 value from `signer`'s namespace using a string key."},"write_config((uint256,uint256,(string,address)[],(string,bytes32)[],(string,uint256)[]))":{"notice":"Write a typed config under `msg.sender`'s namespace."},"write_config_as((uint256,uint256,(string,address)[],(string,bytes32)[],(string,uint256)[]),address,bytes,bool)":{"notice":"Write a typed config under `signer`'s namespace, authorized by signature."}},"notice":"Canonical EVM registry for typed, chain-specific constants. Same CREATE2 address on every EVM chain so downstream init code stays __OFF_CHAIN__hash_config((uint256,uint25.details: Frontends and signing libraries use this to confirm their local digest computation matches the on-chain one before requesting a wallet signature.ERROR CODES:      - `EmptyKey()` if any entry key is the empty string.      - `KeyTooLong(key, length)` if any entry key exceeds 32 bytes.

returns.digest: The digest that `write_config_as` will verify against.

read_address(address,bytes32).details: Prefer this overload inside constructors to avoid string allocation costs.ERROR CODES:      - `KeyNotSet(signer, key)` if no value has ever been written for this (signer, key).      - `KeyTypeMismatch(signer, key, current_value_type, expected_value_type)` if the key is stored under a different type.

params.signer: Namespace owner whose value is being read.

read_address(address,string).details: STRING vs BYTES32 KEY OVERLOADS:      Prefer string overloads for off-chain calls and inspection. Prefer the `bytes32` overload inside constructors to avoid string allocation costs.ERROR CODES:      - `KeyNotSet(signer, key)` if no value has ever been written for this (signer, key).      - `KeyTypeMismatch(signer, key, current_value_type, expected_value_type)` if the key is stored under a different type.      - `EmptyKey()` if `key` is the empty string.      - `KeyTooLong(key, length)` if `key` exceeds 32 bytes.

params.signer: Namespace owner whose value is being read.

read_bytes32(address,bytes32).details: Prefer this overload inside constructors to avoid string allocation costs.ERROR CODES:      - `KeyNotSet(signer, key)` if no value has ever been written for this (signer, key).      - `KeyTypeMismatch(signer, key, current_value_type, expected_value_type)` if the key is stored under a different type.

params.signer: Namespace owner whose value is being read.

read_bytes32(address,string).details: ERROR CODES:      - `KeyNotSet(signer, key)` if no value has ever been written for this (signer, key).      - `KeyTypeMismatch(signer, key, current_value_type, expected_value_type)` if the key is stored under a different type.      - `EmptyKey()` if `key` is the empty string.      - `KeyTooLong(key, length)` if `key` exceeds 32 bytes.

params.signer: Namespace owner whose value is being read.

read_uint(address,bytes32).details: Prefer this overload inside constructors to avoid string allocation costs.ERROR CODES:      - `KeyNotSet(signer, key)` if no value has ever been written for this (signer, key).      - `KeyTypeMismatch(signer, key, current_value_type, expected_value_type)` if the key is stored under a different type.

params.signer: Namespace owner whose value is being read.

read_uint(address,string).details: ERROR CODES:      - `KeyNotSet(signer, key)` if no value has ever been written for this (signer, key).      - `KeyTypeMismatch(signer, key, current_value_type, expected_value_type)` if the key is stored under a different type.      - `EmptyKey()` if `key` is the empty string.      - `KeyTooLong(key, length)` if `key` exceeds 32 bytes.

params.signer: Namespace owner whose value is being read.

write_config((uint256,uint256,(string,ad.details: TIMESTAMP SEMANTICS:      - `config.timestamp` must be <= `block.timestamp` (no future-dated writes).      - `config.timestamp` must be strictly greater than the prior timestamp recorded for every (signer, key) being touched.      - A key's value AND its type are both mutable across writes — the signer is trusted with full authority within their namespace, gated only by strict-monotonic timestamps.EMITTED EVENTS:      - `AddressWritten(signer, key, value)` per address entry.      - `Bytes32Written(signer, key, value)` per bytes32 entry.      - `UintWritten(signer, key, value)` per uint entry.ERROR CODES:      - `EmptyConfig()` if the payload has no entries across all three types.      - `ChainIdMismatch(signed_chain_id, block_chain_id)` if `config.chain_id != block.chainid`.      - `TimestampInFuture(signed_timestamp, block_timestamp)` if `config.timestamp > block.timestamp`.      - `StaleConfig(signer, key, signed_timestamp, prev_timestamp)` if the timestamp does not strictly increase.      - `EmptyKey()` if any entry key is the empty string.      - `KeyTooLong(key, length)` if any entry key exceeds 32 bytes.

params.config: Config payload — chain id, signer-attested timestamp, and entries by type.

write_config_as((uint256,uint256,(string.details: RELAYED AUTHORIZATION:      - Anyone may call this — `msg.sender` has no authority over the resulting state.      - All entries are written under `signer`, not under `msg.sender`.      - The relayer cannot alter the payload without invalidating the signature.EIP-712 DOMAIN:      - name:               "ChainConfig"      - version:            "1"      - chainId:            `block.chainid`      - verifyingContract:  ChainConfig address      The signed `Config` also carries `chain_id`; the contract rejects payloads where `config.chain_id != block.chainid`.      This double-binding catches mismatches between payload creation and the wallet's signing context.EMITTED EVENTS:      - Same as `write_config`.ERROR CODES:      - `InvalidSignature(signer, digest, signature)` if ECDSA or EIP-1271 verification fails.      - All error codes from `write_config` apply.

params.config: Config payload — chain id, signer-attested timestamp, and entries by type.
params.is_eip1271: `true` for contract signers (Safe / DAO / EIP-1271); `false` for EOA and EIP-7702 ECDSA.
params.signature: EIP-712 signature over the digest returned by `__OFF_CHAIN__hash_config`.
params.signer: Namespace owner whose entries are being written (the EIP-712 message signer).

DOMAIN_SEPARATOR().notice: EIP-712 domain separator bound to this deployment.

__OFF_CHAIN__hash_config((uint256,uint25.notice: Compute the EIP-712 digest of a `Config` payload for off-chain signing.

read_address(address,bytes32).notice: Read an address value from `signer`'s namespace using a `bytes32` key.

read_address(address,string).notice: Read an address value from `signer`'s namespace using a string key.

read_bytes32(address,bytes32).notice: Read a bytes32 value from `signer`'s namespace using a `bytes32` key.

read_bytes32(address,string).notice: Read a bytes32 value from `signer`'s namespace using a string key.

read_uint(address,bytes32).notice: Read a uint256 value from `signer`'s namespace using a `bytes32` key.

read_uint(address,string).notice: Read a uint256 value from `signer`'s namespace using a string key.

write_config((uint256,uint256,(string,ad.notice: Write a typed config under `msg.sender`'s namespace.

write_config_as((uint256,uint256,(string.notice: Write a typed config under `signer`'s namespace, authorized by signature.
